    """

    Thought 3: The average of left and right weights can offer insights into combined weight.
    Thought 4: The absolute difference between left and right distances can capture distance differences.
    Thought 5: Weight per unit distance for left and right sides can provide density information.
    Thought 6: Calculate the square of weight differences to potentially reveal non-linear relationships.
    Thought 7: The square root of absolute differences in distances can provide insights into distance variations.

    New Feature 4: weight_ratio | weight_ratio = Left-Weight / Right-Weight
    New Feature 5: weight_ratio_inv | weight_ratio_inv = Right-Weight / Left-Weight

    New Feature 9: right_density | right_density = Right-Weight / Right-Distance

    New Feature 11: square_root_distance_diff | square_root_distance_diff = np.sqrt(distance_difference)
    
    Drop irrelevant or redundant features that don't contribute to task.
